name: Readiness during model warm-up

injection.description: Start a fresh engine replica from cold (model weights load + warm-up) in a pool under traffic and observe the rollout until it serves.

expected_gateway_semantics: The replica's readiness is false throughout model load/warm-up (deployment-contract warm-up-aware readiness); the gateway routes no traffic to it before its warm signal holds.
The startup-probe budget covers worst-case model load, so liveness never kills a loading replica — no restart loops.
Capacity ramps only when the replica reports warm; until then the pool serves on existing replicas.
expected_client_visible_behavior: No client-visible errors or latency degradation attributable to the cold replica at any point in its warm-up.

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: stays 0 for the new backend during load/warm-up, transitions to 1 exactly once when warm
0 on the new replica until readiness true, then rises as traffic arrives (canonical key via name_mapping)

metrics_that_must_not_move.expectation: stays 0 for the warming replica — the probe budget prevents liveness-kill loops
no error_class=upstream_error increase attributable to traffic reaching the cold replica

abort_condition: Abort the injection if the warming replica restarts even once, or if any request is routed to it before its readiness is true.
